# Infrastructure Services

## Overview

TUM provides comprehensive infrastructure services for all departments and organizational units through the IT Service Center (ITSZ) and the Leibniz Supercomputing Centre (LRZ). These services include virtual servers, hardware hosting, client management, web services, and software licensing.

## Server Infrastructure

### LRZ Virtual Servers

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "LRZ Virtual Server Infrastructure"
flowchart TB
    subgraph LRZ["LRZ Data Center"]
        subgraph VirtInfra["Virtual Infrastructure"]
            Win["Windows Server"]
            Linux["Linux Server<br/>(SLES, Debian)"]
        end
        HA["High Availability"]
        Backup["Backup Services"]
        Security["Security Zone"]
    end

    subgraph Management["Management Options"]
        Self["Self-Managed"]
        Managed["LRZ-Managed"]
    end

    VirtInfra --> HA
    VirtInfra --> Backup
    VirtInfra --> Security
    Management --> VirtInfra
```

LRZ provides virtualized high-availability server instances:

| Aspect | Options |
|--------|---------|
| **Operating Systems** | SLES, Debian, Windows Server |
| **Management** | Self-managed or optional LRZ management (OS installation and maintenance) |
| **Availability** | High-availability infrastructure |
| **Location** | LRZ security zone |

### Server Housing & Cluster-Knoten-Housing

LRZ offers attended housing for your own Linux servers or clusters:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Location** | LRZ data center (secured environment) |
| **Hardware Maintenance** | Handled by LRZ (hardware operation) |
| **OS Maintenance** | Handled by LRZ |
| **Applications** | Customer responsibility |

### Windows Server Update Service (WSUS)

LRZ provides WSUS for Windows computers in the Munich Scientific Network (MWN):

- Centralized Microsoft Windows updates for all MWN systems
- Managed update distribution
- Network-efficient patching

### LRZ Compute Cloud

Infrastructure-as-a-Service based on OpenNebula:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Model** | IaaS (Infrastructure-as-a-Service) |
| **Platform** | OpenNebula |
| **Use Case** | Flexible resource scaling for peak loads |
| **Billing** | Based on resource consumption |

### Infrastructure Decision Guide

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Choosing the Right Infrastructure"
flowchart TB
    Start["Need Infrastructure?"]
    Start --> Q1{"Resource-intensive<br/>computing?"}

    Q1 --> |Yes| LRZ["LRZ HPC<br/>Resources"]
    Q1 --> |No| Q2{"Need physical<br/>hardware control?"}

    Q2 --> |Yes| Housing["LRZ Server<br/>Housing"]
    Q2 --> |No| Q3{"Standard web/db<br/>server needs?"}

    Q3 --> |Yes| VM["LRZ Virtual<br/>Server"]
    Q3 --> |No| Q4{"Flexible scaling<br/>needed?"}

    Q4 --> |Yes| Cloud["LRZ Compute<br/>Cloud"]
    Q4 --> |No| Consult["Contact IT Service<br/>Center for Guidance"]
```

## Web Services

### Internet Domains

Domain registration at TUM is restricted to TUM departments, associations, and organizations that operate exclusively or primarily for TUM.

#### Domain Application Process

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Domain Request Process"
flowchart TB
    Need["Need a Domain?"]
    Need --> Type{"Domain type?"}

    Type --> |"Under tum.de<br/>or external"| Form["Submit application form<br/>via TUM secure wiki portal"]
    Type --> |"Subdomain under<br/>faculty domain"| Prodekan["Contact Prodekan<br/>Informationsmanagement"]

    Form --> Review["Review against<br/>Domain Allocation Guidelines"]
    Review --> Q1{"Hosted in MWN?"}

    Q1 --> |Yes| Approved["Domain activated"]
    Q1 --> |"No (external hosting)"| CIO["Requires CIO approval"]
```

| Requirement | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| **Eligibility** | TUM departments, associations, TUM-managed organizations |
| **tum.de domains** | Apply via application form on secure wiki portal |
| **Faculty subdomains** | Contact the Prodekan Informationsmanagement of your school |
| **External hosting** | Requires specific justification and mandatory CIO approval |
| **Guidelines** | Must follow "Richtlinien zur Vergabe von Domains" and "Richtlinien für Webserver" |

::: {.callout-tip}
## TYPO3 as Alternative
TUM offers a centralized TYPO3 content management platform with special terms of service. Consider this option before requesting a domain -- contact the TYPO3 team for details.
:::

### Managed Web Servers

LRZ provides managed web server hosting:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Target** | Departments, institutes, projects |
| **Domain** | Custom domain names |
| **Management** | LRZ handles server infrastructure |

### Website Setup

Options for creating web presence:

1. **TUM TYPO3 Platform**: Centralized CMS with TUM design templates (recommended)
2. **Managed Webserver**: LRZ-hosted custom sites
3. **Self-Hosted**: On your own virtual or housed server

### TUM Web-Styleguide

The TUM Web-Styleguide provides official design guidelines for TUM online systems:

- Official colors and typography
- Layout templates
- Accessibility requirements
- Brand compliance

## Client Management

### Managed Windows PC (TUM-PC)

IT Service Center and LRZ jointly provide managed Windows PC services:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "TUM-PC Management Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Management["Central Management"]
        AD["Active Directory"]
        SCCM["SCCM"]
    end

    subgraph Definition["Organization Defines"]
        PCTypes["PC Types"]
        Software["Software Collections"]
    end

    subgraph Clients["Managed Clients"]
        AdminPC["Admin PCs"]
        Lab["Lab PCs"]
        Office["Office PCs"]
    end

    Definition --> Management
    Management --> Clients
```

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **PC Types** | Define different configurations (admin, lab, office) |
| **Software Collections** | Standardized software packages |
| **AD Integration** | Computers joined to Active Directory |
| **SCCM Deployment** | Automatic software installation and updates |

### Managed Apple Computer (MWN-MAC)

LRZ Client Management for Apple provides:

- Managed macOS workstations in MWN
- Reduced administrative overhead
- Lifecycle management tools
- Centralized configuration

### Client Management with Baramundi

Some TUM departments use Baramundi for client management:

- Supplementary client management software
- Cross-department networking and configuration support
- Contact IT Service Center for contract coordination with other TUM users

### TeamViewer

Remote desktop support tool for administrators:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Remote access to user computers for support |
| **Usage** | User runs a special TeamViewer file to enable remote session |
| **Licensing** | Licenses must be purchased locally by faculties or departments |
| **No Campus License** | There is no university-wide TeamViewer license |

::: {.callout-important}
## TeamViewer Licensing
TeamViewer licenses are NOT covered by a campus-wide agreement. Each faculty or department must procure its own licenses independently.
:::

## Software & Hardware

### Framework Agreements & Campus Licenses

LRZ and TUM have established framework agreements (Rahmenverträge) with major hardware and software vendors:

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Procurement without additional competitive bidding (VOL compliance) |
| **Cost** | Free or significantly reduced prices |
| **Availability** | Many common software packages |
| **Compliance** | Properly licensed for academic and work use |

**Where to find details:**

| Resource | URL |
|----------|-----|
| **Specific Framework Agreements** | Available on the internal IT wiki |
| **Free/Discounted Software** | [wiki.tum.de/display/software](https://wiki.tum.de/display/software) |

Common software available through campus licenses:

- Microsoft products
- Development tools
- Scientific software
- Security software

### Large Equipment Coordination (CIP/WAP)

The IT Service Center coordinates DFG funding programs for IT equipment:

- **Großgeräte** (large equipment)
- **CIP** (Computer Investment Program -- Computerinvestitionsprogramm)
- **WAP** (Workstation Investment Program -- Arbeitsplatzrechnerprogramm)

### Data Carrier Disposal

TUM provides secure disposal of data storage media through a certified process:

#### Accepted Media

| Media Type | Disposal Method |
|------------|-----------------|
| **Hard Drives** | Must be removed from computers before e-waste disposal |
| **Magnetic Tapes** | Secure mechanical destruction |
| **DVDs/CDs** | Shredding |
| **USB Drives** | Secure destruction |
| **Memory Cards** | Secure destruction |

#### Disposal Process

- **Certified Company**: Cintas performs on-site mechanical destruction
- **Security Level**: DIN 32757-1, Security Level 4
- **Method**: Locked collection containers with narrow slots; when full, destroyed on-site by mobile high-capacity shredder

#### Collection Locations

| Location | Address | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| **Campus Garching** | Boltzmannstr. 3 (RBG Informatik) | Permanent collection |
| **Weihenstephan** | Maximus-von-Imhof-Forum 3 (ITW) | Permanent collection |
| **TUM City Center** | Arcisstr. 21 (Wertstofflager) | **Tuesdays 9:30 - 12:00 only** |
| **Klinikum rechts der Isar** | Multiple internal collection points | Contact facility management |

::: {.callout-important}
## Data Security
Never dispose of data carriers through regular waste or standard e-waste bins. Devices often contain personal data recoverable with specialized software. Always use TUM's certified disposal containers.
:::

## Summary

| Service | Best For | Contact |
|---------|----------|---------|
| **LRZ Virtual Servers** | Standard infrastructure, production servers | LRZ |
| **Server Housing** | Custom hardware, special requirements | LRZ |
| **LRZ Compute Cloud** | Flexible, scalable workloads | LRZ |
| **Managed TUM-PC** | Standardized Windows workstations | IT Service Center |
| **MWN-MAC** | Managed Apple workstations | LRZ |
| **Managed Webserver** | Web hosting | LRZ |
| **Internet Domains** | Web presence | IT Service Center |
| **TYPO3 Platform** | TUM-branded websites | TYPO3 Team |
| **Campus Licenses** | Software procurement | [wiki.tum.de/display/software](https://wiki.tum.de/display/software) |
| **CIP/WAP** | Equipment funding | IT Service Center |
| **Data Disposal** | Secure media destruction | Collection locations above |
